Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class FrameGenerator

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Generates Apple II video frames by coordinating bitmap rendering, memory access, and system status. Produces annotated frames for downstream consumers (e.g., NTSC post-processing, GUI display).

public class FrameGenerator : IFrameGenerator
Inheritance
object
FrameGenerator
Implements
IFrameGenerator
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Architecture: This implementation acts as a facade/coordinator, delegating the actual pixel rendering work to an IDisplayBitmapRenderer while managing the lifecycle and annotation of frame buffers.

Frame Annotation: After rendering, this class annotates the frame with display mode metadata (IsGraphics, IsMixed) by setting properties on IFrameProvider. This allows downstream consumers (NTSC renderers, display adapters) to access mode information without requiring their own ISystemStatusProvider reference.

Thread Safety: Not inherently thread-safe. Frame generation should be called from a single thread (typically the emulator thread or a dedicated rendering thread). The underlying IFrameProvider handles thread-safe buffer swapping between renderer and consumer.

Constructors

FrameGenerator(IFrameProvider, IDirectMemoryPoolReader, ISystemStatusProvider, IDisplayBitmapRenderer)

Initializes a new instance of the FrameGenerator class.

public FrameGenerator(IFrameProvider frameProvider, IDirectMemoryPoolReader memReader, ISystemStatusProvider statusProvider, IDisplayBitmapRenderer renderer)

Parameters

frameProvider IFrameProvider

Provider for double-buffered frame storage. Supplies writable frame buffers and manages buffer swapping after rendering completes.

memReader IDirectMemoryPoolReader

Direct memory access for reading video RAM (main and auxiliary banks). Used by the renderer to fetch character data, graphics pixels, etc.

statusProvider ISystemStatusProvider

System status provider for reading soft switch states (video mode, page selection, character set, etc.). Used to determine rendering behavior.

renderer IDisplayBitmapRenderer

The bitmap renderer implementation that converts video memory into displayable pixels. Typically LegacyBitmapRenderer or a future optimized/NTSC renderer.

Exceptions

ArgumentNullException

Thrown if any parameter is null. All dependencies are required for frame generation.

Methods

AllocateRenderContext()

Allocates and initializes a render context for frame generation.

public RenderContext AllocateRenderContext()

Returns

RenderContext

A RenderContext containing the frame buffer, memory access, and system status required for rendering a frame.

Remarks

This method creates a render context that packages together all the dependencies needed for frame rendering:

  • Frame buffer - The writable bitmap where pixels will be rendered
  • Memory access - Direct access to video memory (main and auxiliary)
  • System status - Current soft switch states (video mode, page selection, etc.)

The allocated context is typically short-lived, used for rendering a single frame and then discarded. For performance, the underlying frame buffer may be reused through a double-buffering mechanism managed by the IFrameProvider.

This method should be called once per frame, typically in response to a VBlank event or refresh timer tick (~60 Hz).

RenderFrame(RenderContext)

Renders the current Apple IIe video state into the provided context's frame buffer.

public void RenderFrame(RenderContext context)

Parameters

context RenderContext

The RenderContext containing the frame buffer, memory access, and system status. Typically obtained from AllocateRenderContext(). As a reference type, the context's invalidation state is shared across all references.

Remarks

This method invokes the configured renderer (typically IDisplayBitmapRenderer) to convert the current video memory contents and mode settings into a displayable bitmap. The rendering process:

  1. Examines the system status to determine active video mode (text/graphics, mixed, etc.)
  2. Reads video memory from the appropriate pages (main/auxiliary, page 1/2)
  3. Applies character ROM lookups for text modes or color generation for graphics modes
  4. Writes the resulting pixels to the context's frame buffer
  5. Invalidates the context to prevent reuse (implementation detail)

This method should complete quickly (ideally under 16ms for 60 fps) to maintain smooth frame rates. The actual rendering work is delegated to the renderer implementation, which may employ various optimizations (dirty rectangle tracking, scanline caching, etc.).

Context Lifetime: After this method completes, the provided context is invalidated and cannot be reused. Attempting to access properties or call methods on the context after this method returns will throw InvalidOperationException. Allocate a new context for each frame via AllocateRenderContext().
